title: rubus rivularis var. horriduliformis
intro: Rubus rivularis var. horriduliformis, a lesser-known variety of the raspberry family, is a type of bramble native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-aging properties. Rubus rivularis var. horriduliformis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine formulations, such as tinctures and ointments.
